code_skeleton.models:
# SQLAlchemy models generated by Baxel

from sqlalchemy import Column, String, Integer, DateTime, ForeignKey, Boolean
from sqlalchemy.orm import relationship, declarative_base

Base = declarative_base()

class Users(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'users'
    # User authentication and profile storage

    id = Column(String(36), primary_key=True, nullable=False)
    email = Column(String(255), nullable=False)
    password_hash = Column(String(255), nullable=False)
    created_at = Column(DateTime, nullable=False)

class Auditlogs(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'auditlogs'
    # Storage for Auditlog records

    id = Column(String(36), primary_key=True, nullable=False)
    user_id = Column(String(36), nullable=False, ForeignKey('users.id'))
    created_at = Column(DateTime, nullable=False)

    __tablename__ = 'smarthomesystems'
    # Storage for Smarthomesystem records

    id = Column(String(36), primary_key=True, nullable=False)

    created_at = Column(DateTime, nullable=False)

critical_vulnerabilities: Lack of explicit database write-queue or rate-limiting for high-frequency telemetry
Single point of failure on the PostgreSQL instance
No circuit-breaker configuration around external API integrations

chaos_scenarios.failure_description: High concurrent write operations lock database transactions, exhausting the connection pool.
chaos_scenarios.impact_analysis: HTTP 500 errors on stateful write endpoints, causing clients to drop requests or timeout.
chaos_scenarios.mitigation_strategy: Implement connection pooling limits (e.g., PgBouncer), read replicas for SELECT operations, and client-side exponential backoff retries.

chaos_scenarios.failure_description: An external integration or database is temporarily unavailable during an API request.
chaos_scenarios.impact_analysis: Endpoints depending on third-party APIs hang and time out, blocking event loop processing.
chaos_scenarios.mitigation_strategy: Integrate the Circuit Breaker pattern with fallback responses, and queue requests asynchronously via a broker/Redis.

chaos_scenarios.failure_description: Sudden traffic spike bypasses cache or hits cold cache paths, triggering slow relational database joins.
chaos_scenarios.impact_analysis: Severe latency degradation across all list and fetch endpoints, degrading overall system response.
chaos_scenarios.mitigation_strategy: Pre-warm cache for popular entities, use Redis with write-through pattern, and optimize index coverage on foreign keys.

hardening_checklist: Configure PgBouncer or connection pool sizes matching framework worker limits.
Add a Redis cache layer for high-read dashboard endpoints.
Wrap external HTTP client calls in a circuit-breaker (e.g., tenacity or pybreaker).
Set up structured alert thresholds for DB CPU utilization and connection counts.
